title: Multiplatform Plant Metabolomics Analysis Protocol for Arabidopsis thaliana
author: Akila Wijerathna Yapa

steps.title: Weigh approximately 25 mg of freeze-dried...
steps.instruction: Weigh approximately 25 mg of freeze-dried Arabidopsis thaliana tissues into bead beating tubes. Record the exact weights. Keep samples frozen. °C

steps.title: 25 µL of crude solution of...
steps.instruction: 25 µL of crude solution of samples are added to 25 µL of 1 mM internal standards.

steps.title: Derivatised amino acids are monitored using a fluorescence detector
steps.instruction: Derivatised amino acids are monitored using a fluorescence detector. OPA-derivatised amino acids were detected at 340 ex and 450 em nm from 1-18 min, and FMOC-derivatised amino acids at 260 ex and 325 em nm from 18-21 min.

steps.title: Instrumentation Instrument
steps.instruction: Instrumentation Instrument : Thermo Scientific Vanquish Core UHPLC with FLD detector Column: Agilent Zorbax Extend C-18 column 3.5 um, 4.6 x 150 mm (PN: 763953-902) Guard Column: Phenomenex SecurityGuard Gemini C18 (PN: AJO-7597) Detector FLD 1: OPA-derivatised amino acids detected at 340 ex and 450 em nm Detector FLD 2: FMOC-derivatised amino acids detected at 260 ex and 325 em nm Thermo Scientific Vanquish Core UHPLC with FLD detector

steps.title: Derivatisation Aliquot FMOC reagent, OPA reagent,...
steps.instruction: Derivatisation Aliquot FMOC reagent, OPA reagent, Borate buffer, Iodoacetic acid and Mobile phase A into HPLC vials. Take note of the position in the HPLC autosampler. Derivatisation is performed as previously published [Valgepea et al 2017]. The following steps are programmed in a high-performance autosampler.

steps.title: 1 µL of sample (which has...
steps.instruction: 1 µL of sample (which has been diluted 1:1 with internal standard), is added into 3 µL of 0.5% (v/v) 3-mercaptopropionic acid (Sigma M5801) in borate buffer (0.4 N, pH 10.2, Agilent PN: 5061-3339), mixed and incubated for 20 s at 4 °C , to reduce free cystines.

steps.title: UHPLC parameters The UHPLC gradient
steps.instruction: UHPLC parameters The UHPLC gradient : %s %s Column Temperature: 37 ºC Flow rate: 1.8 mL/min

steps.title: To alkylate reduced cysteines, 1 µL...
steps.instruction: To alkylate reduced cysteines, 1 µL of 120 mM iodoacetic acid (Sigma, I4386) in 140 mM NaOH is added, mixed and incubated for 20 s at 4 °C .
1.5 µL of OPA reagent is then added to derivatise primary amino acids. The reaction was mixed and incubated for 20s at 4 °C

steps.title: FMOC reagent (1 µL) is added,...
steps.instruction: FMOC reagent (1 µL) is added, mixed and incubated for 20 s at 4 °C to derivatise the secondary amino acids.

steps.title: The pH is then lowered by...
steps.instruction: The pH is then lowered by adding 50 µL of Mobile phase A (pH 7). The whole 57.5 µL are then injected to the UHPLC.

steps.title: Quantifications are based on standard curves...
steps.instruction: Quantifications are based on standard curves derived from serial dilutions of a mixed amino acid standard. The upper and lower limits of quantification are 1000 and 1.95 µM, respectively. Chromatograms are integrated using Chromeleon software. Chromatogram

steps.title: To the 500uL samples prepared by...
steps.instruction: To the 500uL samples prepared by DD and AP, add 5uL of 500uM 13C sorbitol. Vortex

steps.title: An injection volume of 5 µL...
steps.instruction: An injection volume of 5 µL was utilized for each sample, with a flow rate maintained at 0.3 mL/min throughout the procedure. The mobile phases consisted of Mobile Phase A: 7.5 mM Tritbutylamine adjusted with acetic acid to a pH of 4.95, and Mobile Phase B: 100% acetonitrile. The chromatographic gradient was established as follows: from 0 to 8 minutes, 2% B; 8 to 22 minutes, a gradient increase from 2% to 20% B; 22 to 32 minutes, further increased to 27% B; 32 to 47 minutes, elevated to 50% B; a sharp rise to 98% B at 47 to 48 minutes, held constant at 98% B until 54 minutes, and then reduced back to 2% B from 54 to 60 minutes. Total analysis time: 60 min Flow: 0.3 mL /min Injection volume: 5ul Oven temperature: 45 °C

steps.title: Dry aliquots of this extract, by...
steps.instruction: Dry aliquots of this extract, by adding 10µl aliquots into a glass insert and drying in a speed vacuum.

steps.title: Before analysis, perform a final quick...
steps.instruction: Before analysis, perform a final quick dry down by adding 25µl methanol as samples must be completely dry before derivatising with methoxyamine and BSTFA + 1%TMCS.

steps.title: Derivatise with methoxyamine (meox) (30mg/ml in...
steps.instruction: Derivatise with methoxyamine (meox) (30mg/ml in pyridine) and BSTFA + 1%TMCS.

steps.title: Add 25ul meox solution and incubate...
steps.instruction: Add 25ul meox solution and incubate at 37 °C for 2 hours. Centrifuge for 10 seconds.

steps.title: Add 25uL BSTFA+ 1%TMCS reagent and...
steps.instruction: Add 25uL BSTFA+ 1%TMCS reagent and incubate at 37 °C for 30 minutes. Leave at room temperature for 2 hours before injecting in GCMS system.

steps.title: GC-MS analysis was performed on a Shimadzu GC/MS-TQ8050 NX system
steps.instruction: GC-MS analysis was performed on a Shimadzu GC/MS-TQ8050 NX system. 1 µL of derivatised sample was injected into the GC inlet set at 280 °C in split mode of 1:10. Chromatographic separation was achieved using an Agilent DB-5 ms capillary column (30 m × 0.25 mm × 1 µm). Oven conditions were set at 100 °C starting temperature, held for 4 min, then ramped at 10 °C/min to 320 °C and held for 11 min. Helium was used as the carrier gas at a flow rate of 1 mL/min. Shimadzu GC/MS-TQ8050 NX system

steps.title: Compounds were fragmented by electron impact...
steps.instruction: Compounds were fragmented by electron impact (EI) ionization and analysed in full scan and MRM mode using the Shimadzu Smart Metabolites Database. ( https://www.shimadzu.com/an/gcms/metabolites/index.html )

steps.title: A high-quality matrix was manually curated...
steps.instruction: A high-quality matrix was manually curated using the Shimadzu LabSolutions Insight GCMS program (v.3.7 SP3, Shimadzu Corporation), where metabolite targets were removed from the dataset if they were not present in all samples.

steps.title: Data acquisition was performed using negative...
steps.instruction: Data acquisition was performed using negative ionization mode, and all analyses were executed on a Shimadzu 8060 LC-MS/MS system. Instrument : Shimadzu 8060 LC MS-MS Column: Phenomenex Gemini NX-C18 3µm x 150mm x 2mm (Part No. 00F-4453-B0) fitted with Phenomenex SecurityGuard column Gemini-NX C18 4 x 2.0mm ID (AJ0-8367). Shimadzu 8060 LC MS-MS System

steps.title: MS/MS Settings Acquisition settings
steps.instruction: MS/MS Settings Acquisition settings: negative ionisation mode Nebulizing Gas flow: 3 L/min Heating gas flow: 10 L/min Drying gas flow: 10 L/min Interface temperature: 300 °C Desolvation line temperature: 250 °C

steps.title: LC gradient protocol %s %s LC Gradient
steps.instruction: LC gradient protocol %s %s LC Gradient

steps.title: CCM method compound list The Central Carbohydrate Metabolic Network
steps.instruction: CCM method compound list The Central Carbohydrate Metabolic Network. Metabolites targeted in LC-MS/MS in this protocol are underlined. Multiple reaction monitoring (MRM) transitions (Q1/Q3) List for identification and quantification of CCMs. %s %s

steps.title: Add approximately 200 μl of 0
steps.instruction: Add approximately 200 μl of 0.5 mm silica glass beads to each sample. Prechill the bead beater (OMNI Bead Ruptor Elite NE486LUA) to -1 °C with ethanol and dry ice.

steps.title: Add 500 μl MQ water with 500 nM AZT
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl MQ water with 500 nM AZT. Vortex mix.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.4 ml chloroform to the samples. Vortex mix. Sonicate in an ice bath for 10 min. ALiquot 500 μl of samples for GC-MS open profiling (Step 23).

steps.title: Add 1 ml methanol to the ground samples
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml methanol to the ground samples. Vortex mix.

steps.title: Extract tissue samples in the bead...
steps.instruction: Extract tissue samples in the bead beater using the following settings: Speed: 7.00 m/s Pulses: 3 x 45 sec Interval: 30 sec between pulses

steps.title: Freeze dry the samples overnight
steps.instruction: Freeze dry the samples overnight.

steps.title: Evaporate methanol in the samples using...
steps.instruction: Evaporate methanol in the samples using a vacuum concentrator until about 400 μl of liquid (water fraction) remains. This step ensures low levels of methanol in preparation for freeze drying.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16,000 RCF for 5 minutes at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16,000 RCF for 5 minutes at 4 °C . Transfer approximately 1 ml of the aqueous layer to new 2 ml Eppendorf tubes, avoiding the interface layer containing proteins.

steps.title: Let stand for 10 minutes on ice
steps.instruction: Let stand for 10 minutes on ice. Centrifuge at 16,000 RCF at 4 °C for 5 minutes, then transfer 200 μl into HPLC glass vial insert. Samples can be used directly for LCMS and HPLC analysis, or stored at -80 °C freezer. Schematic diagram of Multiplatform Plant Metabolomics Analysis Protocol for Arabidopsis thaliana 1. Extraction and Purification of Intracellular Metabolites 2. HPLC — Amino Acid Analysis 3. LC-MS/MS — Central Carbon Metabolism (CCM) 4. GC-MS/MS — Open profiling—amino acids, organic acids, sugars, fatty acids

steps.title: Reconstitute in 200 μl of 2% acetonitrile solution (5x concentrated)
steps.instruction: Reconstitute in 200 μl of 2% acetonitrile solution (5x concentrated).
